prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Alright, here's the truth: the task isn't getting easier while you avoid it, and you're not going to suddenly feel ready. Ready is a myth. You just start.

You've already done the hardest part—carrying this around all morning. That weight drops the second you begin. Not when you finish. When you *begin*.

So set a timer for 25 minutes. Do a messy, imperfect first pass. You can fix it later. You can hate it later. But right now, just start.

Go. You've got this.
